comparison_note: All comparisons use completed days: yesterday (2026-07-19) is the run_date. Today is excluded as it is an incomplete day. Cell C Recharges compares yesterday against same-day-last-month and same-day-two-months-ago, plus MTD-vs-prior-MTD (same day-of-month cutoff, so periods are equal length).

flags: 0 recharges recorded for 2026-07-19 — total outage safety net triggered. Likely cause: data pipeline lag, not a real zero-transaction day — VW_CELLC_RECHARGES currently has no rows past 2026-07-17 (max transaction date), i.e. 2 days behind the 2026-07-19 run_date. Needs data-team follow-up to confirm the load has simply not caught up yet.
2026-07-19 recharge count (0) is >20% below same-day-last-month (2026-06-19: 18,267) and same-day-two-months-ago (2026-05-19: 14,799) — consistent with the same data lag.
2026-07-19 revenue (R0) is >20% below same-day-last-month (R276,874) and same-day-two-months-ago (R224,339) — consistent with the same data lag.
Same-day last-month vs two-months-ago count: up 23.4% (18,267 vs 14,799) — exceeds 5% threshold.
Same-day last-month vs two-months-ago value: up 23.4% (R276,874 vs R224,339) — exceeds 5% threshold.

Current MTD window (2026-07-01 to 2026-07-19) is affected by the same load lag — the underlying data only extends to 2026-07-17, so current MTD count/value likely understate the true month-to-date figures by roughly 2 days' worth of transactions. Read the MTD comparisons above with that caveat; they did not individually cross the 10% threshold, but that may be masking the shortfall.
Spread of 12,114 across subscriber tables — MERGE (465,595) vs DIM_SUBSCRIBERS (453,481) — exceeds the 15-record threshold.
29,889 SIMs with no usage in over 60 days — significantly exceeds the 3,000 threshold.
